hypothesis: If generative models are retrained on chemically accurate Kekulized GEOM-Drugs data with corrected valency calculations, then the generated molecules will exhibit geometries that are closer to GFN2-xTB-optimized references (smaller Δr, Δθ, Δφ, and lower ΔE_relax) than models trained on the original data, because chemically accurate valency handling reduces structure-generation errors.
